Current Workbook Approach: Retain direct GL account-code fields on departments for now.
Decision Needed: Replace with an effective-dated mapping table only when store-specific or multi-system mappings are required.
Current Decision / Guardrail: GL account-code fields will remain on the department record.

Decision Needed: Confirm whether reporting-only parent departments are required in the first release.
Current Decision / Guardrail: Parent level reporting departments will be used, identified by `postable` = false.

Current Workbook Approach: Relevant fields are consolidated on products.
Decision Needed: Reintroduce a publication_details extension only if bibliographic depth or ONIX integration justifies it.

Current Workbook Approach: Stored as name fields on products.
Decision Needed: Decide whether publishers, manufacturers, and purchasing vendors share a future party model.

Current Workbook Approach: POS and inventory quantities are integers in this workbook.
Decision Needed: Change to decimals only when weighted or bulk merchandise is in active scope.

Current Workbook Approach: pos_discounts can reference a future promotion_id.
Decision Needed: Design promotion definitions and application-participant records before implementing advanced promotions.

Current Workbook Approach: Phase 3 Product Requests and later POS records use nullable customer_reference strings; no customers table or customer_id FK is included in v1.
Decision Needed: Design the Customer domain, reusable tax exemptions, notifications, deposits, and customer holds before introducing customers.id.
Current Decision / Guardrail: Opaque customer references are accepted for v1 and must not be treated as stable Customer identity.

Current Workbook Approach: Core store and session fields are included.
Decision Needed: Decide whether thresholds and behavioral settings belong on stores, a store_configurations table, or policy tables.

Current Workbook Approach: receipt_sequence is stored on completed transactions.
Decision Needed: Choose whether the locked next sequence is maintained on stores or in a dedicated sequence table.

Current Workbook Approach: stock_balances stores total unavailable quantity; ledger records status changes.
Decision Needed: Add inventory_status_balances if current damaged/inspection/RTV quantities must be queried without ledger aggregation.

Current Workbook Approach: Ledger movement types anticipate transfer_in and transfer_out.
Decision Needed: Design transfer documents, in-transit ownership, receipt, and discrepancy workflow before adding tables.

Current Workbook Approach: Return disposition and ledger movement anticipate RTV.
Decision Needed: Design RTV authorization, shipment, and vendor-credit documents before adding tables.

Current Workbook Approach: Used variants, inventory units, trade credit, and cash movements provide prerequisites.
Decision Needed: Design seller intake, offer, acceptance, payout, and acquisition records separately.

Current Workbook Approach: Accounts and entries support the current baseline only.
Decision Needed: Add replacement, transfer, expiration, and legal-policy fields only when those workflows are defined.
Design export batches and corrective exports only after the target accounting integration is known.

Current Workbook Approach: Allocations store original quantity; remaining quantity derived from append-only conversion/release events; fulfilment is a separate Product Request Fulfilment fact.
Decision Needed: Confirm exact event schema and cached counters at scaffold time.
Current Decision / Guardrail: Accepted under OD-007 / ADR-0015; do not add received or fulfilled allocation statuses.

Current Workbook Approach: product_requests.product_id required; requested_description removed as identity substitute; four request types.
Decision Needed: Confirm non-customer resolution columns vs resolution events at scaffold.
Current Decision / Guardrail: Accepted under ADR-0015; planning default is columns on product_requests.
